findings.message: The compose interpolation `APP_WIDGET_API_URL: "${APP_WIDGET_API_URL}"` references a variable that is not declared in `.env.example`. `.env.example` defines APP_ENVIRONMENT, APP_LOG_LEVEL, APP_SLO_*, APP_DATABASE_URL, GRAFANA_ADMIN_PASSWORD, and APP_ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL, but has no APP_WIDGET_API_URL entry. Because base.yml is composed by every environment (dev, test, staging, prod), the unset variable will interpolate to an empty string everywhere, breaking DEV->CI->STAGE->PROD parity for this setting.
findings.suggestion: Declare APP_WIDGET_API_URL in the framework-managed block of `.env.example` (e.g. `APP_WIDGET_API_URL=`) so the compose interpolation has an authoritative default and the variable is documented across all environments.

raw_text: [{"path":"infra/compose/base.yml","line":11,"severity":"high","message":"The compose interpolation `APP_WIDGET_API_URL: \"${APP_WIDGET_API_URL}\"` references a variable that is not declared in `.env.example`. `.env.example` defines APP_ENVIRONMENT, APP_LOG_LEVEL, APP_SLO_*, APP_DATABASE_URL, GRAFANA_ADMIN_PASSWORD, and APP_ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL, but has no APP_WIDGET_API_URL entry. Because base.yml is composed by every environment (dev, test, staging, prod), the unset variable will interpolate to an empty string everywhere, breaking DEV->CI->STAGE->PROD parity for this setting.","suggestion":"Declare APP_WIDGET_API_URL in the framework-managed block of `.env.example` (e.g. `APP_WIDGET_API_URL=`) so the compose interpolation has an authoritative default and the variable is documented across all environments."}]
